sourceMovie.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: John Wick: Chapter 2 (2017) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Chad Stahelski's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum (2019) is the natural next step after John Wick: Chapter 2: same action DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Equalizer 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of John Wick: Chapter 2 who respond to its action will find The Equalizer 3 (2023) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Run All Night
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason John Wick: Chapter 2 fans keep landing on Run All Night (2015) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Extraction 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Extraction 2 draws from the same action well as John Wick: Chapter 2, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2023 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Memory
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Memory (2022) captures the action appeal of John Wick: Chapter 2 without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts Memory at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The International
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of John Wick: Chapter 2 — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into The International (2009). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Dead Pool
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Dead Pool (1988) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps John Wick: Chapter 2 rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Nighthawks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Nighthawks (1981) is the kind of film John Wick: Chapter 2 fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: John Wick
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: John Wick shares John Wick: Chapter 2's gift for action — the 2014 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Polar
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes John Wick: Chapter 2 compelling — the action, the crime — shows up in Polar (2019) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

faq.question: What should I watch after John Wick: Chapter 2?
faq.answer: Try John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum, The Equalizer 3, Run All Night—each shares DNA with John Wick: Chapter 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is John Wick: Chapter 2?
faq.answer: John Wick: Chapter 2 (2017) is best described as Action, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is John Wick: Chapter 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "John Wick Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
